paper.title: VecFormer: Towards Efficient and Generalizable Graph Transformer with Graph Token Attention
paper.abstract: Graph Transformer has demonstrated impressive capabilities in the field of graph representation learning. However, existing approaches face two critical challenges: (1) most models suffer from exponentially increasing computational complexity, making it difficult to scale to large graphs; (2) attention mechanisms based on node-level operations limit the flexibility of the model and result in poor generalization performance in out-of-distribution (OOD) scenarios. To address these issues, we propo

memory_insight.compression_quantization_methods: Vector Quantization (VQ) and Graph Token Attention
memory_insight.before_after_comparison: VecFormer reduces memory usage by 30% and improves inference speed by 40%

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing a two-stage training paradigm with codebook-based reconstruction and graph token attention can significantly reduce memory usage and improve model generalization.
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, VecFormer can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices with 8-16 GB RAM, but may require further optimization for real-world applications.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
